contribution.title: Local well-posedness of the Cauchy problem in GR
contribution.description: Proved that the Einstein equations admit a well-posed initial-value formulation in harmonic coordinates.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Foundation of numerical relativity. Every initial-value solver in the field traces back to this result.

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Rigorous PDE methods on the geometry of spacetime; pay close attention to function spaces and energy estimates.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: What is the appropriate function space?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Mathematical rigor sometimes requires assumptions that physicists wave at.

reference.citation: Foures-Bruhat, Y. (1952). Theoreme d'existence pour certains systemes d'equations aux derivees partielles non lineaires. Acta Math. 88, 141.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Designing initial-data and constraint-satisfaction algorithms for future numerical relativity work.
how_to_apply.cautions: Numerical methods must respect the constraint equations, not just the evolution equations.
